name: DAPI (4′,6-Diamidino-2-phenylindole dihydrochloride)
striking_fact: DAPI photobleaches rapidly under UV — even 30 seconds on the UV transilluminator before imaging will kill your signal.

hazard_statements: H317 May cause an allergic skin reaction

sds_facts.storage: Store at −20°C in the dark (amber or foil-wrapped tube). Stock solutions in water or PBS are stable for months when frozen and light-protected.

bench_knowledge: DAPI is a potent DNA dye and suspected mutagen — always wear gloves; do not mouth-pipette.
Protect from light at every step: use amber tubes, wrap in foil, minimize time under bench lighting. Even brief UV exposure before imaging causes irreversible photobleaching.
Standard stock: 1 mg/mL in sterile water (2.84 mM). Working concentration for live cells: 1–5 µg/mL; for fixed cells: 0.5–2 µg/mL. Dilute in the dark immediately before use.
DNA-bound DAPI fluoresces ~20× brighter and blue-shifts: excitation ~360 nm, emission ~460 nm. Unbound DAPI in solution emits at ~500 nm (greener) — background baseline indicates incomplete washing.
For live-cell imaging, use a cell-permeable variant (regular DAPI enters most cells but wash well). For fixed cells, any DAPI works after permeabilization.
Store aliquots in foil-wrapped amber tubes at −20°C. Stock solution is stable ≥6 months. Minimize freeze-thaw cycles (< 10 recommended for stock solutions).
